description: After a massive accounting scandal revealed $1.2 billion in inflated profits, Toshiba initiated a radical restructuring plan in 2015 involving thousands of layoffs. The Japanese public debated whether this 'drastic medicine' was necessary to save the conglomerate or if it represented a loss of traditional corporate ethics.

key_events.description: Independent investigation confirms systematic profit inflation.
Toshiba announces 6,800 job cuts in visual products division.
Public outcry over senior management severance packages.
Market stabilizes but public trust remains at record lows.

calibration_notes: Useful for measuring how initial support for corporate 'fixing' collapses when the 'cost' (layoffs) impacts the workforce.
